edges.justification: The text states that the Gauss-Seidel method is a special case of the Jacobi method, indicating that the convergence properties of the Gauss-Seidel method are derived from those of the Jacobi method.
The text indicates that the weighted Jacobi method extends the Jacobi method just as SOR extends Gauss-Seidel iteration, suggesting a hierarchical relationship.
The text discusses conditions under which the roots of a quadratic polynomial relate to stability criteria, indicating a connection between the properties of the quadratic equation and stability.
The Lax-Wendroff method requires the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the Jacobian matrix for stability analysis, indicating a dependency on eigenvectors.
The Lax-Wendroff method requires finding the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the Jacobian matrix, which are essential for deriving boundary conditions in systems of equations.
The text indicates that the multigrid method can utilize various iterative methods for the smoothing stage, including SOR as an extension of the Gauss-Seidel iteration.
The text indicates that SOR can serve as a preconditioner for the conjugate gradient method, suggesting a relationship where the conjugate gradient method uses SOR as a preconditioning technique.

The text indicates that finding the characteristic normal form of a system involves determining the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the Jacobian matrix, thus establishing a dependency between eigenvectors and eigenvalue problems.
The text discusses the iterative solution of large systems of algebraic equations, indicating that finite difference methods may utilize conjugate gradient methods for practical computations.
The text indicates that multigrid methods are introduced in the context of iterative solutions for large systems of algebraic equations, which are often encountered in finite element modeling.
The text indicates that there has been a significant shift towards iterative methods, including conjugate gradient methods, in the context of finite element modeling of elliptic problems.

The text discusses the finite volume method in the context of numerical techniques and mentions modified equation analysis as a new section, implying that modified equation analysis is relevant to the study and application of finite volume methods.

The text discusses the practical application of numerical methods, indicating that multigrid methods are key topics introduced in the context of iterative solutions, which are relevant for methods like the finite volume method.

The text indicates that the finite volume method is part of the broader context of numerical methods, which includes the use of iterative methods like the conjugate gradient method for solving large systems of equations.
The text suggests that the Jacobi method can be used as an iterative method to find a vector close to the solution in the context of the conjugate gradient method.
